== Notes for [[Item:Q1181|Q1181]] ==

<p style="text-align: left;" dir="ltr">&nbsp;</p>
<p><p style="font-size: 16px; font-family: sans-serif; white-space: normal; word-spacing: 0px; text-transform: none; font-weight: 400; color: #202122; font-style: normal; orphans: 2; widows: 2; margin: 0.5em 0px 1em; letter-spacing: normal; background-color: #ffffff; text-indent: 0px; font-variant-ligatures: normal; font-variant-caps: normal; -webkit-text-stroke-width: 0px; text-decoration-thickness: initial; text-decoration-style: initial; text-decoration-color: initial;">Haplogroup O is associated with populations which speak<span>&nbsp;</span><a style="text-decoration: none; background: none transparent scroll repeat 0% 0%; border-radius: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word;" title="Austric languages" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Austric_languages">Austric languages</a>. The following is a phylogenetic tree of language families and their corresponding<span>&nbsp;</span><a style="text-decoration: none; background: none transparent scroll repeat 0% 0%; border-radius: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word;" title="Single-nucleotide polymorphism" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Single-nucleotide_polymorphism">SNP</a><span>&nbsp;</span>markers, or<span>&nbsp;</span><a style="text-decoration: none; background: none transparent scroll repeat 0% 0%; border-radius: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word;" title="Haplogroup" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haplogroup">haplogroups</a>, sourced mainly from<span>&nbsp;</span><a style="text-decoration: none; background: none transparent scroll repeat 0% 0%; border-radius: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word;" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haplogroup_O-M175#CITEREFEdmondson2007">Edmondson 2007</a><span>&nbsp;</span>and<span>&nbsp;</span><a style="text-decoration: none; background: none transparent scroll repeat 0% 0%; border-radius: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word;" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haplogroup_O-M175#CITEREFShi2005">Shi 2005</a>. This has been called the "<a class="mw-redirect" style="text-decoration: none; background: none transparent scroll repeat 0% 0%; border-radius: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word;" title="Father Tongue Hypothesis" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Father_Tongue_Hypothesis">Father Tongue Hypothesis</a>" by<span>&nbsp;</span><a style="text-decoration: none; background: none transparent scroll repeat 0% 0%; border-radius: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word;" title="George van Driem" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/George_van_Driem">George van Driem</a><span>&nbsp;</span>(<a style="text-decoration: none; background: none transparent scroll repeat 0% 0%; border-radius: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word;" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haplogroup_O-M175#CITEREFvan_Driem2011">van Driem 2011</a>). It does not appear to account for<span>&nbsp;</span><a class="new" style="text-decoration: none; background: none transparent scroll repeat 0% 0%; border-radius: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word;" title="Haplogroup O-M176 (page does not exist)" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Haplogroup_O-M176&amp;action=edit&amp;redlink=1">O-M176</a>, which is found among Japanese, Korean, and Manchurian males.</p>
<p><div class="clade" style="font-size: 16px; font-family: sans-serif; white-space: normal; word-spacing: 0px; text-transform: none; font-weight: 400; color: #202122; font-style: normal; orphans: 2; widows: 2; letter-spacing: normal; background-color: #ffffff; text-indent: 0px; font-variant-ligatures: normal; font-variant-caps: normal; -webkit-text-stroke-width: 0px; text-decoration-thickness: initial; text-decoration-style: initial; text-decoration-color: initial;">&nbsp;</div>
<p><p style="text-align: left;" dir="ltr">&nbsp;</p></p>

REFERENCE_NUMBERS:
6716162EF01341EFBC0B9822EF79916CDA62
[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
